title: polypodium cambricum subsp. serrulatum
intro: Polypodium cambricum subsp. serrulatum, also known as the Welsh polypody or serrulate polypody, is a subspecies of fern native to the British Isles and other parts of Europe. This plant has been used in traditional herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic effects. The health benefits of Polypodium cambricum subsp. serrulatum include reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing, as well as exhibiting potential antimicrobial properties. In herbal preparations, the plant is typically dried and used in infusions or tinctures, often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal effects.
